user_query:
We’re standing up a local-first, persistent memory for our multi-agent assistant. Please read the briefs in input/scenario.md, input/predicate_guidelines.json, and input/episodes.yaml, then design and produce a working memory plan with concrete artifacts:

1) Write output/config.json with a minimal local JSON-storage configuration that uses keyword-only search (embeddings set to noop), and include graph decay settings (decayHalfLifeDays, archiveThreshold, deleteThreshold). Also include a predicateSchemas section that covers at least these three predicates with the specified rules:
- preferred_language: single, conflictPolicy=supersede, normalize=lowercase_trim
- spoken_languages: multi, dedupPolicy=corroborate
- salary: single, conflictPolicy=require_review, normalize=currency

2) Write output/memories.json as an array of at least 15 atomic memory entries derived from input/scenario.md across at least two distinct agents. Each entry should include: id (string), agent (string), text (string), category (e.g., finding|decision|fact|insight|task|event|preference), importance (0.0–1.0). At least 5 entries should include a claim object { subject, predicate, value }. Create a memory graph by adding links: each memory’s links field (array of other memory ids) should reflect meaningful relationships; ensure at least 10 total link references across the file and that all links refer to valid ids. Intentionally include one conflicting claim (same subject+predicate but different value) and mark one of the conflicting entries as quarantined (set status: "quarantined" or quarantine: true) to show how conflicts are handled.

3) Write output/episodes.json defining at least two episodes from input/episodes.yaml. Each episode should have: id, name, and memoryIds (3+ ids per episode) that reference ids from output/memories.json.

4) Write output/report.md (600–900 words) explaining: how you transformed the scenario into atomic memories; your linking rationale (why certain memories connect); how you detected and handled the conflicting claim via quarantine; how the decay policy will work in practice and the maintenance cadence (decay, reinforce, consolidate); and at least two example cross-agent search queries with the types of memories they should retrieve.

Use only relative paths. Ensure that all references between files are internally consistent.
